sections.intro:
TheArachnophilesare one of theSquadsfromInto The Breachadded in the Advanced Edition. They consist of theBulk Mech,Arachnoid MechandSlide Mechand are unlockable with four coins.
Arachnophiles rely on the Arachnoid Mech's Arachnoid Injector, an artillery weapon which creates an Arachnoid when killing an enemy. Arachnoids offer an extra layer of strategy to the squad as they can sacrifice themselves to damage and push enemies, allowing them to steamroll missions if they can be consistently spawned.
sections.Strategies:
- The Bulk Mech's ricochets are capable of dealing damage to multiple targets, both directly and through knock damage. The Slide Mech can be used to set up good ricochets.
  - These ricochets often leave some targets at low health, which the Arachnoid Mech can take advantage of to score kills and spawn arachnoids. The arachnoids themselves can finish off low health targets as well.
  - Unlike theBombermechs' bombs, Arachnoids are not temporary units and should be saved for later turns if they are not needed. They are best used to get kills and handle threats that the mechs aren't capable of handling.
  - While arachnoids can block spawns, they tend to be a little more valuable than a single spawn block. Given that newly spawned Vek will often target them and that they block enemy movement, their presence alone can help control the board; it's generally better to let things spawn and use the additional action granted by the arachnoids only when necessary.
  - Pick an island withLeapersfirst or choosePinnacle Roboticsas your first island. Leapers translate to a free Arachnoid and any Pinnacle mission where the bots are free to destroy also grants spiders, letting you steamroll missions without much issues.
  - You can create Arachnoids from almost any unit, even other Arachnoids (but not from other Mechs). This can help your bots get into some really hard-to-reach places.
  - Certain pilots work well in this squad:Kazaaakpleth: Grants the Slide Mech a decent attack which lets it become more useful early-game.Kai MillerandMorgan Lejeune: Boosting the Arachnoid Mech saves having to invest two cores into a damage upgrade, letting you insta-kill psions and generate spiders. Kai on the bulk mech also increases its firepower significantly but is vulnerable to losing the boost.Silica: Lets the Arachnoid Mech shoot twice, using the Slide Mech to make up for not moving.

full_text:
Arachnophiles
“
Research has shown that the remains of defeated Vek can be used to power smaller, but no less deadly Mechs
„
The
Arachnophiles
are one of the
Squads
from
Into The Breach
added in the Advanced Edition. They consist of the
Bulk Mech
,
Arachnoid Mech
and
Slide Mech
and are unlockable with four coins.
Arachnophiles rely on the Arachnoid Mech's Arachnoid Injector, an artillery weapon which creates an Arachnoid when killing an enemy. Arachnoids offer an extra layer of strategy to the squad as they can sacrifice themselves to damage and push enemies, allowing them to steamroll missions if they can be consistently spawned.

Squad Achievements
[
]
Spider Breeding
Spawn 15 Arachnoids in one Island.
Working Together
Area Shift 4 units at once.
Efficient Explosives
Kill 3 Enemies with 1 shot of the Ricochet Rocket.
Strategies
[
]
The Bulk Mech's ricochets are capable of dealing damage to multiple targets, both directly and through knock damage. The Slide Mech can be used to set up good ricochets.
These ricochets often leave some targets at low health, which the Arachnoid Mech can take advantage of to score kills and spawn arachnoids. The arachnoids themselves can finish off low health targets as well.
Unlike the
Bombermechs
' bombs, Arachnoids are not temporary units and should be saved for later turns if they are not needed. They are best used to get kills and handle threats that the mechs aren't capable of handling.
While arachnoids can block spawns, they tend to be a little more valuable than a single spawn block. Given that newly spawned Vek will often target them and that they block enemy movement, their presence alone can help control the board; it's generally better to let things spawn and use the additional action granted by the arachnoids only when necessary.
Pick an island with
Leapers
first or choose
Pinnacle Robotics
as your first island. Leapers translate to a free Arachnoid and any Pinnacle mission where the bots are free to destroy also grants spiders, letting you steamroll missions without much issues.
You can create Arachnoids from almost any unit, even other Arachnoids (but not from other Mechs). This can help your bots get into some really hard-to-reach places.
Certain pilots work well in this squad:
Kazaaakpleth
: Grants the Slide Mech a decent attack which lets it become more useful early-game.
Kai Miller
and
Morgan Lejeune
: Boosting the Arachnoid Mech saves having to invest two cores into a damage upgrade, letting you insta-kill psions and generate spiders. Kai on the bulk mech also increases its firepower significantly but is vulnerable to losing the boost.
Silica
: Lets the Arachnoid Mech shoot twice, using the Slide Mech to make up for not moving.
